brief: Fallen divine aeon whose error created the material world
significance: PKD's theology heavily draws on Sophia as trapped divine wisdom

brief: Blind demiurge who created the flawed material world
significance: PKD identifies the demiurge with the Black Iron Prison's architect

brief: Creator god of the material world; blind or malevolent in Gnosticism
significance: Central to PKD's theology: the false god who maintains the prison

brief: Supreme cosmic power containing all opposites; numerological deity
significance: Referenced in PKD's discussions of cosmic totality beyond good and evil

brief: First emanation of the divine; divine feminine
significance: Part of PKD's engagement with Sethian Gnostic cosmology

brief: 2nd century Gnostic teacher; founder of Valentinian school
significance: PKD engaged deeply with Valentinian theology and its emanation system
Referenced in PKD's discussions of Gnostic cosmology

brief: 2nd century theologian who rejected the Old Testament God
significance: PKD engaged with Marcion's radical dualism between creator and true God
PKD drew on Manichaean cosmic dualism of light versus darkness

brief: Egyptian town where Gnostic library was discovered in 1945
significance: PKD was deeply influenced by the Nag Hammadi texts discovered in his lifetime

brief: Divine mind; first emanation in some Gnostic systems
significance: PKD uses Nous as the cosmic intellect that breaks through

brief: The divine realm of light; totality of divine emanations
significance: PKD contrasts the Pleroma with the kenoma (void) of fallen reality

brief: Cosmic rulers who imprison divine sparks in matter
significance: PKD's concept of political/cosmic oppressors draws on archon mythology
